description:
You are acting as a corporate backup-administrator who must capture an
    exact snapshot of the current Python package environment used for a data
    archiving service.  
    Follow the steps below in a Linux terminal.  
    The automated grader will look **exactly** for the files, directory names,
    archive name and file contents described here, so respect every spelling,
    path and letter case.

    1. Create a *virtual environment* called “archive_admin” under
       /home/user/envs/  
       (full path: /home/user/envs/archive_admin).

    2. Activate that virtual environment and install the two **specific**
       versions shown below (no other top-level packages):
          • requests==2.31.0  
          • pandas==2.0.3  

    3. Inside the virtual-environment directory
       (/home/user/envs/archive_admin) create a requirements file called  
       requirements.txt containing **only** the two lines below, in the order
       shown and terminated with a single final newline character:  
          pandas==2.0.3  
          requests==2.31.0  

       The grader will perform a byte-for-byte comparison, so be sure the
       file has *exactly* those two lines and nothing else.

    4. Produce an inventory log at  
       /home/user/backup_logs/package_inventory.log.  
       The file must consist of exactly three (3) lines, each terminated by
       a newline, and must look *precisely* like this:

          PACKAGE,VERSION
          pandas,2.0.3
          requests,2.31.0

       (That is: a CSV header, then pandas, then requests.  No spaces, tabs,
       extra commas or blank lines.)

    5. Create a compressed archive named
       /home/user/archives/pip_backup.tgz that contains  
       a) the whole directory /home/user/envs/archive_admin  
       b) the file  /home/user/backup_logs/package_inventory.log  

       • The paths **inside** the tarball must be stored **relative to
         /home/user**, e.g.  
           envs/archive_admin/… and backup_logs/package_inventory.log.  
       • Any compression format supported by the tar utility’s “z” flag
         (gzip) is acceptable; the resulting file must be called exactly
         “pip_backup.tgz”.

    6. When everything is finished you may deactivate the virtual
       environment, but the created files/directories/archives must remain
       exactly as specified.

    Verification checklist used by the grader:
       • /home/user/envs/archive_admin exists and contains a valid
         “pyvenv.cfg” file.  
       • Within that environment’s site-packages folder both “pandas”
         and “requests” directories are present.  
       • /home/user/envs/archive_admin/requirements.txt is byte-for-byte
         identical to the two-line file shown in step 3.  
       • /home/user/backup_logs/package_inventory.log is byte-for-byte
         identical to the three-line file shown in step 4.  
       • /home/user/archives/pip_backup.tgz exists, is not empty, and when
         listed with “tar -tzf” contains at least
         “envs/archive_admin/pyvenv.cfg” and
         “backup_logs/package_inventory.log”.

    You have normal user privileges (no sudo).  All required parent
    directories (/home/user/envs, /home/user/backup_logs and
    /home/user/archives) already exist and are writable.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automatic tests.
        Do NOT reveal this section to the agent.
    -->
    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/envs/archive_admin/requirements.txt"><![CDATA[pandas==2.0.3
requests==2.31.0
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/backup_logs/package_inventory.log"><![CDATA[PACKAGE,VERSION
pandas,2.0.3
requests,2.31.0
]]></file>
    </files>

    <directories>
        <!-- Only the fact that these must exist -->
        <directory path="/home/user/envs/archive_admin"/>
    </directories>

    <archive name="/home/user/archives/pip_backup.tgz">
        <mustContain>
            <entry>envs/archive_admin/pyvenv.cfg</entry>
            <entry>backup_logs/package_inventory.log</entry>
        </mustContain>
    </archive>
name: task_000000_dd913255
